pr.title: Bring CObjGetUpVector closer to a match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Simplified HSD_CObjGetLeftVector in src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c by replacing an inline up-vector calculation and fallback block with a direct call to HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up). The PR title says this brings CObjGetUpVector closer to matching, and the diff shows code deduplication that routes left-vector calculation through the existing up-vector helper.
what_changed: In HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, removed a local reimplementation of the up-vector selection logic.

The removed block checked cobj flags, used cobj->u.up when flag bit 1 was set, otherwise tried roll2upvec(cobj, &up, cobj->u.roll), and fell back to the default up vector (0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f) with res = -1.
Replaced that entire inline block with res = HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up).
The later logic remains the same: if res == 0, compute the left vector with PSVECCrossProduct(&up, &eye, left), then normalize/check it with vec_normalize_check.
smart_moves: Reused HSD_CObjGetUpVector instead of duplicating its behavior inside HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, reducing the chance of divergent camera up-vector behavior.
Kept the existing res-driven control flow around PSVECCrossProduct intact, making the change narrowly targeted for matching.
The replacement removes an obviously redundant null-style check on &up, since &up is a local address and cannot be NULL.
decomp_lessons: When matching baselib camera object routines, prefer calling the helper that represents the original abstraction rather than inlining equivalent-looking logic.
Small helper-call substitutions can improve matching even when they reduce source lines substantially.
For CObj vector routines, HSD_CObjGetLeftVector appears to depend on HSD_CObjGetEyeVector and HSD_CObjGetUpVector before deriving the left vector via a cross product.
If a local block duplicates a nearby exported/static helper's behavior, test whether the original code likely called the helper directly.
naming_conventions: Camera object functions use the HSD_CObj prefix, such as HSD_CObjGetLeftVector, HSD_CObjGetEyeVector, and HSD_CObjGetUpVector.
Vector helper naming includes Vec3 for vector storage and PSVECCrossProduct for paired-single vector cross products.
The local variable names in this area are descriptive direction vectors: eye, up, and left.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace duplicated control flow with a direct helper call when the surrounding code only needs the helper's result code and output vector.
Preserve the result variable pattern, here assigning res = HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up), to keep downstream branches unchanged.
Use known helper semantics for fallback handling instead of repeating default-vector initialization locally when trying to match original code structure.
follow_up_queries: Look for other CObj vector routines that inline HSD_CObjGetUpVector behavior instead of calling the helper.
Check whether HSD_CObjGetUpVector itself now matches or still needs cleanup around roll2upvec and default up-vector fallback.
Search for remaining impossible local-address null checks such as &local != NULL in decompiled baselib code.
